data.title: South Korean Won Snaps 2-Day Gain
data.description:
The South Korean won weakened to around 1,375 per dollar, ending two consecutive sessions of gains, as market sentiment turned cautious amid renewed tariff risks.

On Thursday, a US federal appeals court temporarily reinstated President Trump’s sweeping tariffs, overturning a lower court’s ruling that had blocked them just one day earlier.

On the monetary policy front, economic concerns intensified after the Bank of Korea cut its benchmark interest rate to 2.50% and lowered its 2025 GDP growth forecast to a modest 0.8%, signaling a slowdown in economic momentum.

Political uncertainty also weighed, as investors closely watched the upcoming presidential elections on June 3.

Meanwhile, expectations are growing that South Korea’s trade surplus with the US may narrow, as local firms continue ramping up investment in the US market.

Investors now look ahead to a new wave of South Korean economic data, including trade balance, PMI, and inflation figures, set for release next week.

data.title: Shizuoka Gas Establishes US Subsidiary, Completes Shale Gas Acquisition
data.description:
Shizuoka Gas has completed the acquisition of interests in a US shale gas development project from TG Eagle Ford Resources, through its newly established local subsidiary, Shizugas America.

This marks Shizuoka Gas's third overseas subsidiary, following ventures in Singapore and India, with the final acquisition price adjusted to $127 million, according to its bourse filing on Thursday.

Headquartered in Houston, Texas, Shizugas America will manage the project and explore additional business opportunities in the growing US market.

data.title: Market Chatter: US Tariffs to Hit South Korea's Auto Sector Hardest, BOK Warns
data.description:
South Korea's auto industry is likely to be the hardest hit among major sectors by the Trump administration's sweeping tariff measures, Yonhap News reported, citing a Bank of Korea report.

In its newly released analysis, the central bank projected a 0.6% annual decline in overall auto exports, with shipments to the U.S. forecast to drop by 4%.

The projection assumes the continuation of current U.S. tariffs, including a 25% levy on auto-related goods, the report said.

The anticipated impact reflects the industry's heavy reliance on the U.S. market, which accounted for 47% of its total export volume as of 2024, according to the report.

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on May 30
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Friday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

ECONOMY

ISTAT releases Q1 GDP final data (0800 GMT); CPI May flash data (0900 GMT); April producer prices data (1000 GMT).

DEBT

Italian retail savers have shown less interest in a new inflation-linked government bond compared with previous issuances, bourse data showed on Thursday, as lower consumer prices have made returns less attractive.

Treasury offers new 7-year BTP Italia bond due June 4, 2032 to institutional investors only.

COMPANIES

TELECOM ITALIA , POSTE ITALIANE

Poste has finalised the purchase of an additional 15% stake in Telecom Italia (TIM) from France's Vivendi to become the telecoms group's largest investor with a 24.8% stake, a Consob filing showed on Thursday.

Poste Italiane has no short term plans to request changes at TIM's boards of directors and auditors after becoming the main investor in the telecoms group, a regulatory filing showed on Thursday.

Poste Italiane annual general meeting (1200 GMT).

UNICREDIT

Russian investment firm A1 is not participating in the purchase of the Italian bank's Russian business, A1 CEO Alexander Fain told the Kommersant newspaper on Thursday, reacting to an earlier report from the daily.

PIRELLI

Camfin, the holding company of Pirelli's executive vice chairman Marco Tronchetti Provera, bought around 3.4 million Pirelli shares between 26 and 29 May at a weighted average price of between €6.19 and €6.28, rounding up its stake to 27.3% from just under 27%, according to regulatory filings.

BPER , POPOLARE SONDRIO

The European Central Bank (ECB) has requested BPER to submit a plan for the integration of its takeover target Sondrio within six months of acquiring control, including an updated business plan and details on managing the IT systems, BPER said.

STELLANTIS

The Alfa Romeo premium brand is set to postpone the launch of the new version of its Stelvio large SUV as it reassesses its strategies amid tepid demand for electric vehicles, two sources told Reuters on Thursday.

SNAM , ITALGAS

The Italian gas grid operator said on Thursday it had launched an accelerated book building process to sell up to 83.6 million out of a total 109 million option rights related to Italgas' capital increase.

SAFILO

The eyewear maker said on Thursday it had renewed the supply agreement with Kering Eyewear until 2029.

METRIKS AI (MTK.MI)

Software company debuts on Euronext Growth Milan; listing ceremony with CEO Tiziano Cetarini (0615 GMT).

DIARY

Bank of Italy publishes annual report, with keynote speech by Governor and ECB executive board member Fabio Panetta (0830 GMT).

Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni ends mission in Astana (Kazakhstan) and attends "Central Asia-Italy" summit.

Brescia, farmers lobby Coldiretti holds event on "Food, economic engine of development and growth of the Italian system" (1630 GMT); expected attendees include Coldiretti President Ettore Prandini, Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani, Agriculture Minister Francesco Lollobrigida.

Civitavecchia, Industry Minister Adolfo Urso attends conference "Investing in Italy: innovation meets tradition" on the occasion of the docking of the 'Amerigo Vespucci' ship (0900 GMT).

Genoa, 16th edition 'Labour festival' on "Ethics and sustainability at work. Skills, dignity, inclusion in the age of artificial intelligence" continues (0800 GMT); ends on May 31. On Friday expected attendees include Environment and Energy Security Minister Gilberto Pichetto Fratin, Infrastructure and Transport Minister Matteo Salvini, welfare institute INPS President Gabriele Fava, Economy Deputy Minister Maurizio Leo .

((Milan newsroom, e-mail: reutersitaly@thomsonreuters.com))

data.title: Dow Jones Top Financial Services Headlines at 12 AM ET: SEC Dismisses Lawsuit Against Binance | Berkshire ...
data.description:
SEC Dismisses Lawsuit Against Binance

The regulatory agency has been winding down Biden-era enforcement actions against crypto firms during the Trump administration.

----

Berkshire Hathaway Stock Is Slipping. The â€˜Buffett Premium' Is Leaking Out.

Warren Buffett surprised investors at the May 3 Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting with plans to give up the CEO job at the end of 2025.

----

South African Reserve Bank Cuts Main Repo Rate to 7.25%

The South African Reserve Bank cut its main repo rate by 25 basis points to 7.25% on Thursday, as tensions with the U.S. continue amid ongoing global uncertainty.

----

Japan's NTT Unit Plans to Buy Online Bank in $5.1 Billion Deal

The wholly owned mobile-carrier unit of NTT intends to launch a tender offer to purchase shares of SBI Sumishin Net Bank and take it private.

----

Vance Vows to Champion the Crypto â€˜Movement'

Speaking at a bitcoin conference in Las Vegas, the vice president said President Trump would sign a bill that establishes rules for stablecoins.

----

Trump Officials Keep Talking About Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac. Confusion Reigns.

Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac have been under government conservatorship since the 2008 financial crisis.

----

The Fed Forecasts Stagflation

Officials at their May 6-7 meeting reaffirmed the need for a "cautious approach" given increased uncertainty over tariff policies.

----

Seaside Equity Amasses More Than $720 million for New Funds

Seaside Equity Partners is wrapping up two new funds that will add more than $720 million in fresh capital to the San Diego-based firm's investment coffers, according to people familiar with the process.

----

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on mortgage rates, interest in buying a home, UniCredit and more in the latest Market Talks covering financial services.

----

Bank of Montreal Raises Dividend After Rise in Quarterly Earnings

Bank of Montreal bumped up its dividend after earnings in its second-quarter were lifted by strength in its wealth management business.

----

UniCredit to Double Stake in Greece's Alpha Bank to Around 20%

The move deepens UniCredit's partnership with one of Greece's largest lenders at a time when its expansion efforts in Germany and Italy are facing roadblocks.

----

Telegram Set to Raise $1.5 Billion in Bond Issue Despite CEO's Legal Woes

The messaging app is set to raise funds from both new and existing backers.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 12 AM ET: Oil Futures Retreat After Boost From Tariff Ruling | U.S. ...
data.description:
Oil Futures Retreat After Boost From Tariff Ruling

The boost to oil from a U.S. court ruling against President Trump's trade tariffs proves short-lived. WTI settled down 1.5% at $60.94 a barrel, and Brent fell 1.2% to $64.15.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Fall More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories fell by 2.8 million barrels, exceeding estimates for a drop of 600,000 barrels.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Futures Settle Lower

U.S. natural gas futures lost a few cents with a storage build landing close to market expectations and the weather outlook pointing to another week of light demand before summer heat starts to set in.

----

NRG's Stock Is a Top Gainer. How Does It Really Make Money?

The company looks more like a risky energy trader than a simple power producer.

----

Shell to Buy TotalEnergies' Stake in Nigeria Oil Field for $510 Million

The stake increases Shell's interest in the deep-water Bonga field to 67.5% from 55%.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Adani Power, oil futures and more in the latest Market Talks covering energy and utilities.

----

Controversial New York Pipeline Project Is About to Be Revived

Pipeline operator Williams will seek to build two natural-gas supply lines to New York following talks between President Trump and Gov. Kathy Hochul.

----

Trump's Nuclear Orders Could Loosen Radiation Standards. DOGE's Key Role.

The president wants to speed up construction of reactors. But experts are worried about the safety measures that keep the public safe.

----

Oil Giants Face Mix of Climate Pressures at Annual Investor Meetings

One year after Exxon Mobil sued activist shareholders pressing it on climate issues, the company touted that the energy transition is "an opportunity."

----

Why Big Oil Turned to Trump for Help in Africa

Chevron, ConocoPhillips and other companies are locked in a dispute with six Central African countries over fossil-fuel projects.

----

Chevron Gets Narrow License to Preserve Oil Assets in Venezuela

The company can maintain key infrastructure but is barred from importing oil from the South American country.

----

Enel Increases U.S. Renewable Energy Capacity in Swap, $50 Million Cash Deal

The energy company said the deal with Gulf Pacific Power would increase its consolidated renewable capacity in the U.S. by 285 megawatts.

data.title: AI Likely to Continue Driving Long-Term Equity Performance, UBS Says — Market Talk
data.description: AI could continue to drive long-term equity performance despite near-term volatility, UBS global wealth management chief investment office says in a research note. UBS expects global AI spending to reach $360 billion in 2025, up 60%, and further grow to $480 billion in 2026. Recent results from the four leading global cloud platforms showed a solid 24% on-year growth in combined revenue, they note, adding that robust cloud growth should narrow the gap between AI spending and monetization, which is also supported by rising AI adoption rates across industries and company sizes. Despite being bullish on the long-term trend, UBS favors a more balanced exposure between semiconductor and software names in the near term to navigate volatility. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Faizan Zaki, 13, crowned US National Spelling Bee champion
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dallas-area boy spells winning word without hesitation
  • 'Eclaircissement,' synonym for enlightenment, decides contest
  • Champion beats out eight other finalists after 21 rounds

Faizan Zaki, a 13-year-old boy from the Dallas area, won the 97th Scripps National Spelling Bee on Thursday, swiftly nailing the French-derived word "eclaircissement," synonymous with enlightenment, in the 21st round of the contest finals.

He edged out 14-year-old Sarvadnya Kadam, from Visalia, California, who finished in second place after misspelling "Uaupes," a tributary of the Rio Negro in South America, in the 20th round.

Zaki, who was last year's runner-up, had correctly spelled "Chaldee," a dialect of the Biblical Aramaic language, in the 20th round. But under spelling bee rules, Zaki needed to land one more word in a solo round to claim the trophy.

He did so in round 21 by instantly and precisely spelling "eclaircissement" - defined as a clearing up of something obscure. He surprised the audience by giving his answer without taking the customary pause afforded contestants to ask the judges for more information about the word's origins, meaning and pronunciation.

He was crowned champion in a hail of confetti before being joined on stage by his parents and other relatives, and will receive $50,000 in prize money.

Asked what he would do next, Zaki replied, "I'm probably going to stay up the entire night or something."

Zaki, a resident of Allen, Texas, had nearly been eliminated in round 18 when he rushed, and stumbled over, the spelling of "commelina," a genus for some 200 species of dayflowers.

But his two fellow finalists at that point, including Kadam, likewise fumbled their words, leading to a 19th round in which all three boys returned, but only two - Zaki and Kadam - survived to face off in the decisive 20th round.

Sarv Dharavane, an 11-year-old boy from Tucker, Georgia, finished in third place after misspelling "eserine," the antidote of choice for many poisons, with one "s" too many.

Thursday's total roster of nine finalists emerged from three days of competition at a convention center outside Washington, D.C., among 243 contestants aged eight to 14 who advanced from regional competitions across the country.

Challenged with some of the most difficult and least-used words that English has to offer, many young competitors amazed spectators with their ability to produce the correct spellings with poise and precision.

The Scripps media group has sponsored the event since 1925, with three years off during World War Two and one more for the COVID pandemic in 2020.

Most competitors were from the United States, coming from all 50 states. Other spellers came from Canada, the Bahamas, Germany, Ghana, Kuwait, Nigeria, Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands.

data.title: Superloop's Balance Sheet Supports Top-Pick Status — Market Talk
data.description: Superloop retains its status as Morgans analysts' pick of Australian telecom stocks, helped by an ungeared balance sheet that supports a range of options. Morgans analysts tell clients in a note that the stock is close to fair value based on current earnings, but say they see potential upside from strong sales momentum and the balance-sheet options. They note that telecom companies typically can handle net debt of one or two times Ebitda, which indicates Superloop could return capital to shareholders or undertake acquisitions, in which they see the latter as more likely. Morgans raises its target price by 15% to A$3.00 and keeps an add rating on the stock, which is down 1.1% at A$2.67. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0351 GMT - Superloop retains its status as Morgans analysts' pick of Australian telecom stocks, helped by an ungeared balance sheet that supports a range of options. Morgans analysts tell clients in a note that the stock is close to fair value based on current earnings, but say they see potential upside from strong sales momentum and the balance-sheet options. They note that telecom companies typically can handle net debt of one or two times Ebitda, which indicates Superloop could return capital to shareholders or undertake acquisitions, in which they see the latter as more likely. Morgans raises its target price by 15% to A$3.00 and keeps an add rating on the stock, which is down 1.1% at A$2.67. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0317 GMT - IHH Healthcare is poised for long-term growth, backed by solid execution, a strong regional presence, and resilient demand for premium healthcare, says RHB IB analyst Oong Chun Sung in a note. He is upbeat on IHH's organic and inorganic growth strategies, especially after Malaysia scrapped plans for a diagnosis-related group payment system, which removes a key regulatory overhang. DRG is a system that groups hospital cases by diagnosis and treatment, with each group reimbursed at a fixed rate regardless of actual treatment cost. Oong raises IHH 2025-2027 earnings forecasts by 7%, 11% and 11%, respectively, after 1Q earnings beat expectation. RHB raises IHH's target price to MYR9.00 from MYR8.70, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 0.7% lower at MYR6.86.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0228 GMT - Tenaga Nasional is set to benefit from record electricity demand driven by data centers, commercial growth and hot weather, Affin Hwang IB analyst Isaac Chow says in a note. He expects electricity sales to rebound strongly in 2Q amid rising peak demand. Tenaga has completed 21 data-center projects, and 17 are under construction, he notes. Management also sees continued strong interest from data-center developers and aims to secure an additional 10 electricity supply agreements, each with a maximum demand of 150 MW to 200 MW by end-2025, he adds. Chow cuts Tenaga's 2025-2027 earnings forecasts by 4%-8% amid a higher tax rate. Affin Hwang lowers the target price to MYR16.20 from MYR16.70, with a buy rating. Shares fall 1.0% to MYR13.94. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0218 GMT - Bangkok Chain Hospital's tone seems positive on the recent high budget spending by Thailand's Social Security Office, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a research report, as they maintain a buy rating on the stock. Management clarified that this spending won't affect the current "AdjRW>2" rate of THB12,000 per patient, easing investors' concerns over the past few days, the analysts say. SSO's payments to flood victims in September 2024 and earthquake victims in March 2025 raised concerns that the SSO may lack funds to maintain the current rate, they note. However, the brokerage lowers the stock's target price to THB17.50 from THB20.00, based on its 2025 enterprise value/Ebitda multiple of 14.0x. Shares last closed at THB13.80. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0153 GMT - Genting Malaysia's 2Q earnings may be seasonally slower, but 3Q and 4Q are expected to improve on higher spending by customers, Maybank IB analyst Yin Shao Yang says in a note. Genting Malaysia is set to submit its bid for a full casino license in New York City next month, which he estimates could add over MYR0.50 to its target price. Yin cuts Genting Malaysia's 2025-2027 core net profit by 8%, 7% and 6%, respectively, following underwhelming 1Q results. Maybank cuts Genting Malaysia's target price to MYR1.95 from MYR2.08, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 1.1% lower at MYR1.80.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0143 GMT - Malaysia's GDP could grow 4.6% in 2025, up from the existing projection of 4.0%, if a U.S. court ruling invalidating Trump-era tariffs is upheld, MIDF Research says in a note. Improved trade prospects may boost Malaysia's trade performance, especially electrical and electronic exports, it reckons. The benchmark KLCI could rise to 1670 this year under this upside scenario, it says, but warns risks remain if trade tensions escalate or nontariff barriers are introduced. MIDF favors domestic-driven sectors like construction, utilities and property, citing names like Gamuda, Tenaga Nasional and Eco World Development. The KLCI is flat at 1519.23. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0139 GMT - Aroa Biosurgery's conservative revenue guidance implies that the wound-treatment company's elevated growth momentum should continue, according to Wilsons analysts. They write in a note that the guidance is seen as achievable by the market, adding that they anticipate a potentially significant increase in productivity from its Myriad product sales reps in fiscal 2026. They reckon that the stock should trade in line with wound-care peers at three times 2026 revenue. Wilsons lifts its target price 9.3% to A$0.82 and stays overweight on the stock, which is flat at A$0.53. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0134 GMT - China Aviation Oil (Singapore) may benefit from some tailwinds, Phillip Securities Research's Liu Miaomiao says in a report. Profit from its associates is expected to grow at least 12% in 2025, supported by continued rebound of inbound leisure and business travel, the analyst says. Also, the company's volume in trading of jet fuel and other oil products is likely to pick up in 1H, driven by rising demand for jet fuel and sustainable aviation fuel, the analyst says. The brokerage upgrades the stock's rating to accumulate from neutral and raises the target price to S$0.90 from S$0.85 to reflect recent share-price performance and improving financial metrics. Shares last closed at S$0.83. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0127 GMT - Genting Bhd.'s share sell-off appears overdone despite weaker-than-expected 1Q earnings, says TA Securities analyst Tan Kam Meng in a note. The stock is down 21.5% year-to-date. The $10.5 million fine related to illegal gambling at Resorts World Las Vegas has resolved the case, and the U.S. reciprocal tariff has had no noticeable impact on visitor numbers, he notes. Tan cuts Genting's 2025-2026 earnings forecasts by 47%-68% and lowers the target price to MYR3.56 from MYR4.02. TA Securities maintains a buy rating on Genting. Shares are 3.2% lower at MYR3.03. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0105 GMT - IHH Healthcare is poised for continued growth, supported by a planned 28% increase in hospital bed capacity by 2028, mainly in Malaysia and India, Hong Leong IB analyst Chee Kok Siang says in a note. In Singapore and Hong Kong, IHH plans to expand across the care continuum with a focus on ambulatory and primary care, while also aiming to turn around its China operations, he says. Ongoing refurbishments at Singapore's Mount Elizabeth Hospital at Orchard may pressure margins until 2Q, he adds. Management remains open to merger-and-acquisition opportunities, targeting value-accretive assets in Malaysia, India, Turkey, and Europe, he notes. Hong Leong raises IHH's target price to MYR9.06 from MYR9.03 while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 0.7% lower at MYR6.86.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0103 GMT - Web Travel gets a new bull at Citi even though much of the travel sector's recent tariff-related volatility has yet to show up in its financials. Analyst Samuel Seow raises his recommendation to buy/high risk from neutral/high risk, telling clients in a note that he is attracted by the Australian travel booker's growth and risk-reward. He acknowledges that some tariff-related uncertainty could yet show up in Web Travel's numbers, but likes its stabilizing take rates and material market-share growth. Citi raises its target price 20% to A$6.60. Shares are down 1.2% at A$5.11. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0050 GMT - If Ryman Healthcare has a vision for the next three to five years that goes beyond modest improvements in cash generation then management doesn't appear ready to share it with the market, says Forsyth Barr. It had hoped Ryman's FY 2025 result would offer clarity on the path ahead, stating it's "a raison d'etre to own Ryman through what is undoubtedly going to be a prolonged turnaround." Instead, Ryman's FY 2026 guidance disappointed. Ryman expects 1,200 total sales at the midpoint of its guidance range, substantially below Forsyth Barr's estimate of 1,350 ahead of its result. "The lower sales expectation is likely the core driver of Ryman's unwillingness to guide to positive free cash flow for FY 2026--another disappointment," analyst Aaron Ibbotson says. Forsyth Barr retains a neutral call on Ryman. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Alqemam for Computer Systems Wins SAR3 Million Maintenance Services Deal in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Alqemam for Computer Systems (SASE:9558) received a contract worth 2.9 million Saudi riyals from the Municipality of Mahayel Asir Governorate for maintenance support.

The IT management company said Thursday it will service the cameras, displays, and lightning arresters of the Saudi Arabian municipality and its affiliated hubs.

data.title: Fitch Affirms EDP Rating on Business Profile, Revenue Visibility
data.description:
Fitch affirmed on Thursday the BBB long-term issuer default rating of Energias de Portugal (EDP.LS), d/b/a EDP, with a stable outlook.

The rating agency attributed its decision to the Portuguese utility's well-diversified business profile and high revenue visibility from regulated operations, balanced generation and supply businesses integration, and long-term contracted renewable operations.

data.title: SED Industry Chair Resigns
data.description:
Shenzhen SED Industry said Si Yuncong resigned as chair, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Si will no longer hold any position in the telecommunication and electronic products company.

Shenzhen SED Industry shares were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Lost Investment in UroGen Pharma Ltd. (URGN)? Levi & Korsinsky Launches Securities Fraud Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 29, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of UroGen Pharma Ltd. ("UroGen Pharma Ltd.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On May 16, 2025, the FDA stated: "Given that ENVISION lacked a concurrent control arm, the primary endpoints of complete response (CR) and duration of response (DOR) are difficult to interpret. While CR indicates drug activity of UGN-102, it is unclear whether the observed DOR can be attributed to the investigational product or instead reflects the natural history of the disease."

Following this news, the price of the Company's stock dropped on the same day. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/urogen-pharma-ltd-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=150848&wire=1&utm_campaign=60

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. either via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or by telephone at (212)363-7500.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Investors in Civitas Resources, Inc. (CIVI): Protect Your Rights - Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before July 1, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 29, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Civitas Resources, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/civitas-resources-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=150850&wire=1&utm_campaign=11

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Civitas Resources, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 27, 2024 and February 24, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: (i) Civitas was highly likely to significantly reduce its oil production in 2025 as a result of, inter alia, declines following the production peak at the DJ Basin in the fourth quarter of 2024 and a low TIL count at the end of 2024; (ii) increasing its oil production would require the Company to acquire additional acreage and development locations, thereby incurring significant debt and causing the Company to sell corporate assets to offset its acquisition costs; (iii) the Company's financial condition would require it to implement disruptive cost reduction measures including a significant workforce reduction; (iv) accordingly, Civitas's business and/or financial prospects, as well as its operational capabilities, were overstated; and (v) as a result, the Company's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Civitas Resources stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/civitas-resources-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=150850&wire=1&utm_campaign=11 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. data.title: KSH Holdings Returns to Profit in Fiscal H2
data.description:
KSH Holdings booked an attributable profit to owners of SG$526,000 for the fiscal second half ended March 31, against an attributable loss of SG$18.4 million a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Earnings per share came in at SG$0.001 compared with a loss per share of SG$0.0328 in the comparable period.

Revenue jumped 100% year over year to SG$128.1 million from SG$64.1 million, due to strong performance from the construction business.

The company declared a total cash dividend of SG$0.0125 per share for the 12 months ended March 31.

data.title: Baiyunshan Pharmaceutical Gets Chinese Marketing Approval for Cancer API
data.description:
Guangzhou Baiyunshan Pharmaceutical received the approval of China's drug administrator to market active pharmaceutical ingredient sorafenib tosylate.

The drug is used to treat liver, kidney and thyroid cancers, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the pharmaceutical company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: International Financial Advisors Signs $28 Million Credit Agreement
data.description:
International Financial Advisors (DFM:IFA) secured a credit limit of $28 million from a local bank, according to a Thursday release.

The Dubai-listed Kuwaiti investment company said the move will result in an increase in its cash and cash equivalents and liabilities by the same amount.

data.title: DXN Secures AU$5 Million Loan to Fund AU$2.1 Million Property Purchase, Repay Debt; Shares Up 9%
data.description:
DXN secured a AU$5 million loan from iPartners to fund the now finalized AU$2.1 million SDC Darwin property purchase and repay existing debt, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The facility, which started on May 29 and has a term of 18 months, will generate an annual cash flow saving of AU$266,000, the filing added.

The company said the property has been independently valued at AU$10 million, giving an immediate uplift to its net assets.

The facility has enabled the company to pay down AU$2 million of outstanding debt and settle a AU$550,000 warranty payment owed to the SDC Darwin vendor from the original purchase agreement from 2021.

The company's shares rose nearly 9% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Offshore Yuan Set for Weekly Decline
data.description:
The offshore yuan edged lower to around 7.18 per dollar on Friday, heading for a weekly decline as investor caution prevailed amid stalled US-China trade negotiations.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated on Thursday that talks with China are “a bit stalled” and may require direct involvement from President Trump and President Xi Jinping to advance.

While a temporary truce was reached two weeks ago, momentum has since faded.

Market sentiment was further dampened by Washington’s decision to tighten export restrictions, effectively barring certain US firms from selling key technologies to China.

Elsewhere, adding to investor concerns was the US appeals court reinstating President Trump’s reciprocal tariffs, overturning an earlier ruling that had deemed them unlawful.

On the domestic front, investors await upcoming PMI data scheduled for release over the weekend, which is expected to shed light on the economic health of the world’s second-largest economy.

data.title: RBNZ Signals Data-Driven Path Ahead as Rate Cuts Near End
data.description:
New Zealand's interest rates now sit within the 2.5%–3.5% neutral range and future moves will depend on how the economy evolves, as past rate cuts have yet to fully take effect, Reserve Bank Assistant Governor Karen Silk said.

In a Reuters interview, she noted global trade uncertainty remains high but expects the economy to recover, supported by earlier easing that may offset some risks.

The Reserve Bank of New Zealand cut rates by 25bps to 3.25% in its May gathering, signaling the easing cycle may be nearing its end after 225bps in cuts since August. Silk explained further cuts aren’t urgent due to delayed policy impacts and strong commodity exports. “The track for cash rates also indicates that whatever we do is going to be data-dependent,” she said.

On U.S. tariff rulings, Silk added, “There isn't certainty to where these things land… but we do have heightened uncertainties.”

data.title: A Singaporean producer offers Polypropylene (PP Random Copolymer) in Indonesia
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Indonesia reported that a producer based in Singapore is heard to have offered its PP Random Copolymer grade at the USD 1230/mt levels.This offer is for June 2025 shipment on CIF Indonesia Main port basis

data.title: Equinox Resources Receives Exploration Permit for Canada Project; Shares Rise 5%
data.description:
Equinox Resources secured an exploration permit for the Alturas project in Canada from the British Columbia Ministry of Mining and Critical Minerals, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The permit will enable drilling, induced polarization geophysical surveys, geochemical sampling, and low-impact exploration activities, the filing said.

A second application is in progress for the bulk sampling program to evaluate the metallurgy and direct shipping ore potential of the project, per the filing.

Shares rose 5% in afternoon trade Friday.

data.title: Snam Completes Sale of Option Rights for Italgas Shares
data.description:
Snam (SRG.MI) said Thursday it raised 32.5 million euros from the private placement of 83,349,489 option rights for Italgas (IG.MI) shares at 0.396 euro apiece.

Settlement is expected by June 4. After the transaction, the Italian infrastructure company will hold 25,883,804 option rights for the subscription of shares in the Italian gas distributor, with the exercise of the options to be financed using proceeds from the placement.

data.title: Can Fin Homes Raises INR9.8 Billion via Allotment of Debentures
data.description:
Can Fin Homes has raised 9.80 billion Indian rupees through the allotment of non-convertible debentures on a private placement basis, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The debentures carry a coupon rate of 7.24% and are scheduled to mature on May 29, 2028. They are listed on the National Stock Exchange of India.

The issue had a base size of 5 billion rupees and a green shoe option of 4.80 billion rupees.
